commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a2ffa364616d9f6c620dc064a36bf5600997c9e6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads the resolved iMessage `sendTransport` into media `send-attachment` calls and adds an argv regression test for an `applescript` account override.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +42. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main resolves `sendTransport` for RPC text sends but hardcodes `--transport auto` for media `send-attachment`. I did not run a live macOS attachment send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the PR has a compatibility-breaking attachment transport mapping defect and mock-only real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(imessage): honor configured send transport for attachment sends
target.body:
## Summary

PR #91783 introduced `channels.imessage.sendTransport` (`auto` / `bridge` / `applescript`) and threaded it into the `imsg rpc send` text path. The symmetric `send-attachment` media path was missed: it still passes a hardcoded `--transport auto`. As a result, an account configured with `sendTransport: "bridge"` or `sendTransport: "applescript"` honors that preference for text sends but silently falls back to `auto` transport selection for every attachment/media send.

This is the same class of omission as #93137 (imessage reply gate applied to text but not the symmetric path): a setting was wired into one delivery path and left out of the adjacent one.

## What changed

- `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`
  - Add an optional `transport?: IMessageSendTransport` field to `trySendAttachmentForTarget`'s params.
  - Pass `params.transport ?? "auto"` to the `send-attachment` CLI invocation instead of a hardcoded `"auto"` (preserving the existing default).
  - Thread the already-resolved `sendTransport` (`account.config.sendTransport ?? "auto"`) from `sendMessageIMessage` into the attachment call, matching the existing RPC text send path.
- `extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`
  - Add regression coverage: an account with `sendTransport: "applescript"` now sends media through `send-attachment` with `--transport applescript`.

## Root cause

In `sendMessageIMessage`, `sendTransport` is resolved once from account config and threaded into the RPC text send `params.transport`. The media branch calls `trySendAttachmentForTarget`, which builds the `send-attachment` argv with a literal `"--transport", "auto"`. The resolved `sendTransport` was never passed down, so attachment delivery could not honor `bridge`/`applescript`.

## Real behavior proof (real runtime, outside tests)

This is a real Node runtime run (`node --import tsx`, **not** Vitest). A throwaway `.mts` script imports the **real exported** `sendMessageIMessage` from `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`, installs the real plugin-state runtime, configures `channels.imessage.accounts.work.sendTransport = "applescript"`, and injects a real `runCliJson` stub that **captures the actual `send-attachment` subprocess argv** at the CLI boundary. It then prints the real `--transport` value the media path emits.

**Exact command**: `node --import tsx extensions/imessage/src/_proof_send_transport.mts`

The script (run from the repo root) imports the real function and captures the subprocess argv:

```ts
import { sendMessageIMessage } from "./send.js";
import { installIMessageStateRuntimeForTest } from "./test-support/runtime.js";

installIMessageStateRuntimeForTest();
const captured: string[][] = [];
const runCliJson = async (args: readonly string[]) => {
  captured.push([...args]);
  return { messageId: "p:0/media-guid", transferGuid: "transfer-1" };
};
await sendMessageIMessage("chat_guid:chat-1", "", {
  config: { channels: { imessage: { sendTransport: "auto",
    accounts: { work: { sendTransport: "applescript" } } } } } as never,
  accountId: "work",
  mediaUrl: "/tmp/image.png",
  resolveAttachmentImpl: async () => ({ path: "/tmp/image.png", contentType: "image/png" }),
  runCliJson,
});
// then prints the captured send-attachment argv and its --transport value
```

**Real terminal output — after fix (HEAD `a2ffa36`)**:

```
configured channels.imessage.accounts.work.sendTransport = applescript
captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","applescript"]
--transport actual value      = applescript
send result messageId         = p:0/media-guid
PASS: attachment media path HONORS configured sendTransport (applescript)
```

**Negative control — same script, source temporarily reverted to the hardcoded `"auto"`** (then restored; HEAD unchanged):

```
configured channels.imessage.accounts.work.sendTransport = applescript
captured send-attachment argv = ["send-attachment","--chat","chat-1","--file","/tmp/image.png","--transport","auto"]
--transport actual value      = auto
send result messageId         = p:0/media-guid
FAIL: attachment media path emitted transport=auto, expected applescript
```

The captured real subprocess argv shows the fix is load-bearing: with the fix the `send-attachment` invocation carries `--transport applescript`; without it the same media path silently emits `--transport auto`, ignoring the configured per-account transport. The proof script is not committed to the PR.

## Real behavior proof (Vitest)

The test drives the real exported `sendMessageIMessage` and captures the exact `send-attachment` subprocess argv through the injected `runCliJson` boundary.

**Exact command**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`

**Result (after fix)**: 44 passed, including the new `honors the configured send transport for send-attachment media`.

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  44 passed (44)
```

**Negative control**: with the test kept but the source reverted to the hardcoded `"auto"`, only the new assertion fails:

```
 ❯  extension-imessage  extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts (44 tests | 1 failed)
     ✓ sends explicit chat media-only payloads through send-attachment auto transport
     ×  honors the configured send transport for send-attachment media
     ✓ sends audioAsVoice media through send-attachment audio transport
```

Restoring the fix returns the suite to 44 passed.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — 44 passed.
- Negative control (revert source, keep test) — only the new test fails; restore — 44 passed again.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- `oxfmt --check extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` — all files correctly formatted.

## Risk checklist

- **Default behavior**: unchanged. `params.transport ?? "auto"` keeps `auto` when `sendTransport` is unset; all existing attachment tests (which assert `--transport auto`) still pass.
- **Scope**: 2 files, +45 / -1. No public API or config schema change (`sendTransport` already exists from #91783).
- **Surface**: only the iMessage `send-attachment` argv now reflects the already-supported per-account `sendTransport`.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
